dialogue.utterance: Unit 3 dispatched to 143 Sunnyside for possible stroke.
Hello, I'm EMT Jill with the rescue squad. Can you describe what's going on for you today?
I woke up with left side weakness and slurred speech. It's gotten worse over the last few hours.
Ms. Linda, can you tell me what day and where we are right now?
I believe it's Sunday. I'm in Richmond.
Great-can you try to move your left hand for us if it's okay?
Your airway is patent, which is great-can I check your breathing next?
Your breathing is even, at 18 per minute with no signs of respiratory distress.
Strong peripheral pulses with equal strength in both arms, and overall good circulation.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Let me check your pupils-just shine a light, don't worry.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status
dialogue.utterance: You seem alert and aware-can you tell me your name and the year?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Nuchal rigidity
dialogue.utterance: I don't feel any neck pain or stiffness.
Your oxygen saturation is 100%, which is excellent.
Pulse is 94-strong and regular.
Blood pressure is 156 over 102.
Let me test your blood sugar now-this is a quick and routine step.
I need to set up the four-lead EKG now-this is part of our stroke protocol.
The EKG is showing a normal sinus rhythm with no immediate abnormalities.
When did you first start noticing this weakness, and when did it get worse?
I woke at noon with some weakness and went back to sleep. When I woke again at 5, it was worse.
You've had a recent TIA, diabetes, high BP, and kidney disease-correct?
Your family mentioned you're allergic to Tramadol, Lisinopril, and Ansaid.
What was the last thing you had to eat or drink today?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Can you tell me what you were doing when you started feeling the weakness?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: She fell back asleep and didn't notice the worsening until her husband told her.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Pupils are equal and reactive, by the way-just a note.
We'll try to start an IV in your right arm since we'll be using your left side for assessments.
Unfortunately, I wasn't able to place the IV after two attempts due to poor vascular access.
While you don't report pain, can you rate the weird feeling or pressure in your left side from 0 to 10?
It's not painful, just strange. I'd say maybe a 7 in terms of oddness.
Does it feel more intense when you try to move, or is it constant?
It's constant and gets worse after I wake up in the evening primarily on the left.
Any other areas having the same kind of sensation or pressure?
Field impression is consistent with a probable stroke-activating our Stroke/TIA protocol.
Picking you up and bringing you to Johnston Willis, which handles stroke cases quickly.
She indicated a pressure-like headache after the second awakening on the left side.
Blood pressure has dropped slightly to 153 over 94.
Pulse has dipped to 85.
Breathing is still stable at 18 per minute.
Oxygen saturation is now 99%-still within normal range.
No new symptoms-she remains alert, talking, and has a strong Glasgow score of 15.
Weakness and slurred speech are still intact. No change in symptoms during transport.
Non-ambulatory status continues-left side is numb and not functioning as normal.
Chronic conditions include IBS, GERD, diabetes type 2, and stage 2 kidney disease.
ECG monitoring is still set up and running-no new readings to report.

dialogue.micro_intent: retake history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: Any nausea, dizziness, or vomiting since the onset of symptoms?
You take medications for your diabetes and blood pressure-do you remember the names?
They take something for blood pressure and something insulin level-names are unknown.
Remain on Stroke/TIA protocol as instructed. No protocol changes required.
Reiterate-standard transport procedures applied.
Glucose dropped out due to device unavailability, but initial was 384.
All straps on the stair chair and stretcher in use-no concerns for movement.
Pressure headache remains but no additional pain reported otherwise.
Ongoing neurological deficits consistent at onset-left-sided droop and speech.
EKG intact and continuous-again no change noted in baseline.
All patient vitals ongoing meet Stroke Alert standards.
All additions complete-ready and secured for transport.
IV attempt reconfirmed-no success still noted.
Breathing is clear with equal sounds bilaterally.
IV attempt remains unsuccessful due to poor peripheral access.
She is alert, speaking well, and non-painful for the short ride.

dialogue.micro_intent: retake history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: Any new changes to the initial symptoms or body responses?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: You noticed the changes at second awakening-correct?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Yes, and I really didn't feel much until I tried to sit up later.
EKG still running and standard-set to be reviewed upon arrival.
Alarms functional and set-monitoring continuous.
Transport placed in Semi-Fowler's position due to patient condition.

dialogue.micro_intent: retake history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: No additional symptoms reported. Pressures are constant, but no new groupings.

dialogue.micro_intent: retake history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: Left-side numb, right commanded with motor intent-significant deficit noted.
Complete top-to-bottom wearing secured. Safe for transport as planned.
Still indicating 7 on oddness, but no pain, as per previously observed.
Any exacerbation during movement transitions like standing?
No pain report-just aiming to reexamine left-side pressure and full-body impact.
She's non-painful, stable, and ready for immediate imaging and treatment.
All readings invite immediate neurological interventions expected at the receiving facility.
Still Sinus-no deviation from first reading.
Patient comprehends, speaks, has left-side discourse maintaining active trail.
Left foot numb-walking not actionable, no signs of pain.
Reassessments complete-no new symptoms at second check complete.
Pressure on left tends to increase with patient movement but remains the same.
Blood pressure remains consistent despite transport jostling.
Complete symptom trail remains consistent with initial exit.
Receive disposition: non-urgent on scene, high transit priority due to neurological concern.
Safe, secured, and monitored throughout for stroke intervention at arrival.
Final vitals remain consistent. All data ready and reviewed.
Ready for handoff as expected.
Patient alert, GCS 15, no pain reported.
No change, secure, tracked to Johnstone Willis for intervention.
Quick ride still alerting appropriate on arrival.
Provided patient report as per protocol-no issues noted on field.
All hands on board-settin for early care.
